frameworks.name: adaptive evidence accumulation
frameworks.one_line: non-linear integration of evidence tuned to hidden change-points in the environment

frameworks.name: recurrent cortical microcircuit
frameworks.one_line: circuit-level implementation of normative non-linear accumulation

frameworks.name: pupil-linked arousal modulation
frameworks.one_line: arousal gates frequency-specific feedback to sensory cortex

frameworks.name: normative decision computation
frameworks.one_line: optimal accumulation under non-stationary state as motivation

core_question: How does the human brain implement adaptive, non-linear evidence accumulation when the environment can change state?
key_assumptions: maximizing accuracy in changing environments requires non-linear accumulation
MEG indexes decision-related cortical population activity
action-encoding regions reflect the accumulated decision variable
manipulations: visual evidence accumulation in a changing environment
key_findings: behavior and action-planning cortical activity show hallmarks of adaptive accumulation
parietal and frontal decision dynamics are mirrored in visual cortex state
this sensory-cortex modulation is frequency-specific and depends on arousal and expected change probability
interpretations: adaptive accumulation can be realized by recurrent cortical circuit dynamics
decision-related feedback to sensory cortex is itself adaptive to environmental statistics
